ion_action.objective:
B01_CODEX_RUNNER_TIMEOUT_POLICY_001.

Implement a bounded fix for Codex queue timeout policy so cartography, probe, agent-invocation, and design workloads receive realistic runtime budgets and return workload-scoped diffs.

Context proof from B01:
- ION/04_packages/kernel/ion_codex_queue_runner.py already defines DEFAULT_CODEX_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 1800 and MAX_CODEX_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 7200.
- Failed RUNTIME_CARTOGRAPHER run received timeout_seconds = 180 and failed before latest_return.md was produced.
- Failed CONTEXT_CARTOGRAPHER run received timeout_seconds = 30 and failed before latest_return.md was produced.
- The runner itself appears healthy; the shorter timeouts likely come from caller/API invocation policy.

Inspect:
- ION/04_packages/kernel/ion_chatgpt_browser_mcp_connector_contract.py
- ION/04_packages/kernel/ion_agent_invocation_broker.py
- ION/04_packages/kernel/ion_codex_queue_runner.py

Patch only if evidence confirms caller-side timeout override is too low or missing workload-class policy.

Required implementation:
- Add or repair workload-class timeout policy at the connector/broker boundary.
- Avoid 30s/180s budgets for agent/cartography/proof packets.
- Prefer at least 900s for agent/cartography/proof workloads unless caller requests larger up to 7200.
- Preserve no production authority and no live execution authority.
- Require WORKLOAD DIFF in future queued workload return contracts where applicable.
- Do not read secrets.
- Do not deploy.
- Do not push git.
- Do not broaden shell access.
- Run targeted validation only.

Required return sections:
- CONTEXT PROOF
- TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF
- VALIDATION
- RESULT
- WORKLOAD DIFF
- BLOCKERS
- RECOMMENDED NEXT PACKET
